forecast_track.position_description: 455 km East Northeast of Itbayat, Batanes
525 km North of Itbayat, Batanes (OUTSIDE PAR)
800 km North Northwest of Extreme Northern Luzon or vicinity of Zhejiang, China (OUTSIDE PAR)

tcws.areas_raw: Batanes, Cagayan including Babuyan Islands, Isabela, northeastern Quirino (Maddela), Apayao, northern Abra (Tineg, Lagayan, Danglas, San Juan, Lagangilang, Licuan-Baay, Malibcong, Lacub, Daguioman, Dolores, La Paz), Kalinga, eastern Mountain Province (Natonin, Paracelis), eastern Ifugao (Aguinaldo, Alfonso Lista), Ilocos Norte, northern Aurora (Dilasag, Casiguran), and northern/central Catanduanes (Pandan, Caramoran, Bagamanoc, Panganiban, Viga, Gigmoto, Baras, San Miguel).
tcws.potential_impact: Minimal to minor threat to life and property. Highest possible signal mentioned by PAGASA: Signal No. 2.

rainfall.risk: Numerous flooding events likely; landslides likely in moderate to highly susceptible areas.

rainfall.areas: Occidental Mindoro, Antique, and Negros Occidental
rainfall.risk: Localized flooding possible; landslides possible in highly susceptible areas.
Localized flooding possible; landslides possible in highly susceptible areas.
Numerous flooding events likely; landslides likely in moderate to highly susceptible areas.

rainfall.areas: Zambales, Bataan, Palawan, Iloilo, Guimaras, Negros Occidental, Negros Oriental, Zamboanga del Sur, Lanao del Norte, and Lanao del Sur
rainfall.risk: Localized flooding possible; landslides possible in highly susceptible areas.
Numerous flooding events likely; landslides likely in moderate to highly susceptible areas.

rainfall.areas: Pangasinan, La Union, Zambales, Bataan, Pampanga, Bulacan, Metro Manila, Rizal, Cavite, Batangas, Oriental Mindoro, Romblon, Palawan, Aklan, Capiz, Iloilo, Guimaras, Negros Occidental, and Negros Oriental
rainfall.risk: Localized flooding possible; landslides possible in highly susceptible areas.

marine.affected_seaboards_raw: Northern and eastern seaboards of Luzon, and eastern seaboard of Visayas. Very rough seas up to 6.0 m over Batanes, northern/eastern Babuyan Islands, and northeastern mainland Cagayan; up to 5.0 m over remaining mainland Cagayan/Babuyan Islands and Isabela; up to 4.5 m over Aurora, Camarines Norte, northern Camarines Sur, Polillo Islands, Catanduanes, Northern Samar and Eastern Samar seaboards.

cpf_risk_matrix.commodity_affected: Feeds, silo/bulk feeds, live animals, RM transfers
cpf_risk_matrix.main_hazard: TCWS No. 1; strong winds; exposed coastal/upland route risk
cpf_risk_matrix.remarks: Validate road passability, wind exposure, receiving readiness, GPS status, and contractor readiness before release.

cpf_risk_matrix.area_route: Eastern/northern seaboards of Luzon and eastern Visayas
cpf_risk_matrix.main_hazard: Gale Warning No. 3; rough to very rough seas up to 6.0 m
cpf_risk_matrix.remarks: Hold small seacraft/motorbanca-dependent movement; validate larger vessels with Coast Guard, ports, and shipping lines.

cpf_risk_matrix.main_hazard: TCWS No. 1 plus Isabela seaboard exposure
cpf_risk_matrix.remarks: Dispatch only after local route, contractor, and receiving confirmation.

cpf_risk_matrix.area_route: Gerona / Tarlac hub and Central Luzon outbound routes
cpf_risk_matrix.main_hazard: Downstream northbound/eastbound/western corridors may be affected by wind, rain, or marine advisories
cpf_risk_matrix.remarks: Coordinate route validation for affected corridors; update Affected Delivery Updates file.

cpf_risk_matrix.area_route: NCR / CALABARZON / Bataan / Zambales / Bulacan / Pampanga
cpf_risk_matrix.main_hazard: Habagat rainfall/gust watch; possible flooding/visibility reduction
cpf_risk_matrix.remarks: Check flood-prone roads, receiving delays, and route restrictions before release.

cpf_risk_matrix.main_hazard: Monsoon rainfall, flooding/landslide-prone roads, and port disruption risk
cpf_risk_matrix.remarks: Coordinate with contractors, local teams, customers, and port/RORO operators before dispatch.

cpf_risk_matrix.area_route: Mindanao areas under monsoon rainfall/gust watch
cpf_risk_matrix.main_hazard: Gusty conditions and localized flooding/landslide risk
cpf_risk_matrix.remarks: Keep GPS active and report road disruptions immediately.

dispatch_recommendations.reason: TCWS No. 1 and closest approach risk.

dispatch_recommendations.area: Eastern/northern seaboards of Luzon and eastern Visayas
dispatch_recommendations.reason: Gale Warning No. 3 and rough to very rough seas.

dispatch_recommendations.area: Central Luzon, NCR/CALABARZON, Visayas and Mindanao routes
dispatch_recommendations.reason: Habagat/rain/gust exposure and local passability changes.

contractor_advisory: All freight contractors must validate route, weather, road, bridge, and port conditions before dispatch. Keep GPS active, confirm driver/helper readiness, secure cargo, and report flooding, landslides, road closures, port cancellations, low visibility, strong winds, route deviations, vehicle issues, or delivery delays immediately. Do not proceed through unsafe flooded or landslide-prone roads without CPF Logistics clearance.
change_log: Updated to PAGASA Tropical Cyclone Bulletin No. 6 issued 5:00 AM, 09 July 2026.
INDAY weakened from Super Typhoon to Typhoon.
Maximum sustained winds changed to 175 km/h; gustiness changed to 215 km/h; pressure changed to 940 hPa.
Current center moved to 17.8°N, 130.5°E, 925 km East of Northern Luzon.
Weather Advisory updated to No. 11 and Gale Warning updated to No. 3.
reliability_notes: PAGASA is the official source of truth.
Satellite imagery is visual context only and must not override PAGASA values.
Forecast cone is not plotted unless official cone geometry is available.
